Name: National Educational Panel Study - Starting Cohort Grade 5 (2010; SC3)
Official Website: https://www.neps-data.de/Mainpage (for the data)    https://www.lifbi.de/en-us/Start/Research/Large-Scale-Projects/NEPS-National-Educational-Panel-Study (for more information about NEPS in general)
Data Collection Frequency: Yearly (or more than once per year)
Sample Type\/Size [Random Students' Sam: 4974 - The average is really missleading as there are refreshments - please compare the data manual:   https://www.neps-data.de/Portals/0/NEPS/Datenzentrum/Forschungsdaten/SC3/13-0-0/SC3_13-0-0_DataManual.pdf
Data Accessibility [Other]: There are three modes: Download, RemoteNEPS and On-site at the LIfBi // https://www.neps-data.de/Data-Center/Data-Access
Additional notes on the dataset (strengh:
Please notice:  

The participants of the NEPS are usually interviewed closely and tested continuously. Nevertheless testing in NEPS is not repeated for all domains in every year of data collection. For a closer look: https://www.neps-data.de/Portals/0/NEPS/Datenzentrum/Forschungsdaten/Kompetenzen/Overview_NEPS_Competence-Data.pdf. NEPS Starting Cohort Grade 5 (2010; SC3) was surveyed at least once a year until 2022. So the answer below is "12".  After 2022 we switched to a concept of wide-meshed surveying with an outcomesurvey all five years - without testing.
Other information on students (specify)::
The overarching goal of the National Educational Panel Study (NEPS) is to describe and explain the development of competences, educational processes, their conditions, outcomes and consequences over the entire life course. To this, there is a conceptual framework that outlines the subject area in the form of core topics, central questions and theoretically assumed relationships from a transdisciplinary perspective across the life course - in addition to recording the life course with various educational decisions and the consideration of competence development, this includes information on various learning environments (formal and non-formal), information on the individuals themselves and, in particular various educational outcomes . Futher more there are many data collected to explain social inequality regarding gender, migration and social origin (cf.: https://doi.org/10.1007/s11618-023-01156-w).


The data contain 8851 variables that were collected from 2010 to 2022; it should perhaps be emphasised that the students were initially surveyed in the school context (questionnaire after the test in the classroom context). After leaving school, they were switched to an individual field and telephone and oral interviews with a lot of information also on their further life course and educational outcomes.
Additional notes:
If you are looking for specific content: 

https://variablesearch.lifbi.de/?MaxItems=25&AggregateStep=Wave&DisplayLanguage=de&InstrumentIds=&ConceptIds=&CohortIds=%5B3%5D&ProjectIds=%5B1%5D&SearchText=&SelectedCount=25&StageIds=&TargetGroupIds=&Waves=&Y


For more information on regional level: https://www.neps-data.de/Data-Center/Overview-and-Assistance/Regional-Data
Parental Cccupation: Detailed information (e.g. ISCO code) for both parents
Household Information Type:: Administrative (provided by the institution or others)
Other information on parents or househol:
Migration background is measured up to the third Generation + there is also information on language spocken at home, houshold income / number of household members  (at all / > 14 years)

Please notice: Information on houshold is also given in the parent questionnaire. The second parent in the NEPS context is defined as the partner of the  interviewed parent that lives also in the household.
Teacher Information Linkability: Linkable to the ones of students
Other information on teachers (specify):: migration background/mother tongue (spocken with pupils) / further educational training/ class composition
Additional notes .2:
The questionaires for the teachers are comparable to those of NEPS Starting Cohort Grade 9 (SC4) - in part the schools in the sample are the same.

Unfortunately, the participation rate of teachers is not particularly high and there is no information on whether a teacher has ever participated (no identification possible via the panel history)
Other information on the school or class:
classroom equipment / support transition to vocationial training / integration and inclusion /  work within the collegeues / full time school / parental participation 

for more information on regional level: https://www.neps-data.de/Data-Center/Overview-and-Assistance/Regional-Data
Additional notes .3:
The questionaires for the head of the schools / class teacher are comparable to those of NEPS Starting Cohort Grade 9 (SC4) - in part the schools in the sample are the same.

The class composition was requested from the class teacher.

Variables with child care information are collected in the parents questionanire. In some caeses we got anseres from educators. The corresponding questionnaires (PAPI) were passed on from the parents to the educators or the childminders. This means that all information from educators, childminders and institu‐ tion managers (for the first time in wave 4) is directly linked to the target child and there are no identifier variables available for educators, childminders or institutions. Therefore, external child care persons and institutions can only be connected to the child via ID_t and not them‐selves followed through the survey waves.
Additional notes:
"Surveying early child characteristics and development is a particular challenge of NEPS Starting
Cohort 1, taking into account the special situation of investigating infants and young children (no
group testing, limited attentional skills, etc.). In the first three waves, so‐called direct measures
with the child were implemented. They involve measures of basic cognitive abilities as well
as observational measures: habituation‐dishabituation paradigm, parent‐child interaction and
sensorimotor development. All direct measures were administered in the households of the
families, videotaped and coded afterwards. " https://www.neps-data.de/Portals/0/NEPS/Datenzentrum/Forschungsdaten/SC1/10-1-0/NEPS_SC1_DataManual_10-1-0_en.pdf (p 14)
Other information on students (specify):: The overarching goal of the National Educational Panel Study (NEPS) is to describe and explain the development of competences, educational processes, their conditions, outcomes and consequences over the entire life course. To this, there is a conceptual framework that outlines the subject area in the form of core topics, central questions and theoretically assumed relationships from a transdisciplinary perspective across the life course - in addition to recording the life course with various educational decisions and the consideration of competence development, this includes information on various learning environments (formal and non-formal), information on the individuals themselves and, in particular various educational outcomes . Futher more there are many data collected to explain social inequality regarding gender, migration and social origin (cf.: https://doi.org/10.1007/s11618-023-01156-w).

Name: National Educational Panel Study - Starting Cohort Grade 5 (2022: SC8)
Official Website: https://www.lifbi.de/en-us/Start/Research/Large-Scale-Projects/NEPS-National-Educational-Panel-Study (for more information about NEPS in general)
Data Collection Frequency: Yearly (or more than once per year)

Additional notes on the dataset (strengh:
The participants of the NEPS are usually interviewed closely and tested continuously.  NEPS Starting Cohort Grade 5 (2022; SC8) is surveyed at least once a year and currently we plan until 2030. --> So I filled in ~8 +

First data will be avaible approx. end of 2024


"Another unique feature of the new SC8 is the implementation of a school panel, focusing on school development. For this sake, not only the teachers of the target persons and the school head are interviewed but the whole teaching staff of a school. The questions address teacher cooperation, school culture and educators’ own further education. In addition, the school panel is also a part of the (planned) twofold combination with IQB Trends in Student Achievement  by (1) implementing overlapping school samples of IQB Trends 2022 and SC8. Furthermore, (2) IQB Trend tests (reading & mathematics, end of elementary school) are implemented in SC8’s grade five assessment. The design enables to address issues related to the short and long-term impact of students’ educational standard proficiency levels (e.g. minimum vs. optimum standards) at the end of elementary school on the further pathways through secondary school and beyond."  Artelt, C., & Sixt, M. (2023). The National Educational Panel Study (NEPS) - Framework, design, and research potential. Zeitschrift für Erziehungswissenschaft, 26(2), 277-298. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11618-023-01156-w (p 293)
Type of Skills Analysed [Other skills (a: by digital competences and civic literacy + Linkage with IQB Trends in Student Achievement

Name: Evaluaciones Diagnósticas (Gobierno Vasco)
Data Collection Duration: 2 years. They are evaluated for the first time in the 4th year of primary education and again in the 2nd year of secondary education
Additional notes on the dataset (strengh: The Basque Country seems the only Spanish region where educational authorities have applied a relatively limited longitudinal character to evaluating competencies. Students have been evaluated twice along their initial educational trajectory, and two cohorts have gone through this experience so far. On the webpage, they claim that they are the only regional evaluations “that are applied by an external party outside the schools and the system, and therefore, the reliability of the data is much higher” (Arenas & Gortazar, 2024)
Type of Skills Analysed [Other skills (a: learning to learn and to think
Measure Type: [Other]: The measurement seemingly copies the PISA format
Student Information Type: Administrative (provided by the institution or others)
Additional notes: For sure, there is no information about variables. This would require that the data is well documented online. It's not the case. I'm afraid that in the website of the data, there is not a form to formally request the data, which is worrying. This is the reason why some other fields in the form cannot be filled in.
Parental Cccupation: Detailed information (e.g. ISCO code) for both parents

Name: INVALSI population and sample data
Individual Level Longitudinal Design: No, but it is possible to follow students over time thanks to an individual student’s identifier/identification code
Data Collection Frequency: Yearly (or more than once per year)
Sample Type\/Size [Students' Population: INVALSI data have two levels: 1) population data (census, collected on the entire population) – relating to skills, characteristics of the student and family, 2) sample data (representative at a regional level) – relating to skills, characteristics of the student and family + Questionnaires Teacher, school principal
Data Accessibility: On a personal computer without restrictions
Additional notes on the dataset (strengh:
INVALSI data are both population and Sample:
Teacher and Principal Survey Data are collected only on the sample are linkable only to sample data of student test.
Type of Skills Analysed [Literacy (areas: Reading comprehension (narrative and expositive text; grammar and use of language)
Type of Skills Analysed[Numeracy (areas): Numbers, Data and Predictions; Relations and Functions; Space and Figures
Additional notes: INVALSI data have both continuous scores and proficency levels for grades 8, 10 and 13
Other information on students (specify)::
Age of arrival in the Country for students born abroad
Number of siblings (with specification of gender)
Additional notes: Some variables have been present for some years in the past (e.g. attitudes toward education)  but not in the most recent datasets.
Other information on parents or househol:
Language spoken at home
Dialect spoken at home
Additional notes .1:
Place of birth: categories of this variable are large groups (e.g. European Union; Europe extra Union, ...), not specific Country
Parental level of knowledge of the language: not assessed directly, but there is the variable "language spoken at home" that can be a proxy
Teacher Information Linkability: Linkable to the ones of students
Other information on teachers (specify)::
- Attitudes towards national tests
- Teaching Methodology/Strategies
- Attitudes towards Artificial Intelligence and Digital Skills
-Professional development and efficacy
Additional notes .2: Teacher and Principal Survey Data are collected only on the sample are linkable only to sample data of student test.
Other information on the school or class:
- physical infrastructure (e.g. gym, laboratories, 
- educational material (e.g. LIM, educational softwares. )
Additional notes .3: Some information are not in the standardized dataset available (e.g. Type of school public/private), but can be release upon request

Name: Anagrafe Nazionale Studenti - microdata
Individual Level Longitudinal Design: No, but it is possible to follow students over time thanks to an individual student’s identifier/identification code
Data Collection Frequency: Yearly (or more than once per year)
Data Linkability At Individual Level: [: administrative data at the student level, communicated by the schools
Data Accessibility: On a personal computer with a specific interface
Access to Micro-Data [Other]: The school principal and the authorized secretarial staff can enter the SIDI portal and keep updated the data of the students attending the school. To use the portal: you need to be in possession of a valid username for access to the SIDI service; be profiled for the management of the Student Area; have carried out the recognition procedure.
Additional notes on the dataset (strengh:
The administrative data can be linked through the SIDI code (a unique identifier for each student entering the Italian educational system) to the INVALSI data, that contains information on students’ results in the national standardized test scores. The national student registry contains additional information on students that are not registered in the INVALSI data. 
IMPORTANT: Not all the information flagged in the next sections is collected in the same way. There might be inconsistencies since schools are the ones who register this information, and for some information there is not an obligation for schools to communicate them.  Therefore, not all the information indicated in the technical annex is actually available and shareable for research purposes.
Additional notes: Information on the skills investigated are not directly collected through the ANS but can be linked through the SIDI code to the INVALSI data.
Student Information Type: Administrative (provided by the institution or others)
Other information on students (specify):: Town of birth (not of residence); day of absence; student status (attending, dropping out, transferred, home education, attendance at a non-equal school); number of days - weekly time
Additional notes:
Age = year of birth 
University administrative can also access the data and verify the credentials of students with the following information: school year in which the diploma was obtained, school code and related description (school where exams were held and/or attendance), high school course of study, type of qualification achieved (type of diploma), final examination result and grade, honors indicator (yes/no), specific information on the different evaluations of the final exam for the diploma.
Household Information Type:: Administrative (provided by the institution or others)
Additional notes .1: All the information related to student’s household characteristics are linkable to the INVALSI administrative information.
Teacher Information Type: Administrative (provided by the institution or others)
Teacher Information Linkability: Not linkable to the ones of students
Additional notes .2: No information on teachers directly linkable to ANS at the student level. ANS OPEN provides some information on teachers who are aggregated at the province level (see ANS OPEN). However it is possible to link information of (some) students with the subset of teachers questionnaire provided by INVALSI for specific grades (5, 10)
Other information on the school or class: School code (plesso), number of classes per school, schools' self-evaluation reports
Additional notes .3: The information flagged above on school characteristics are not directly present but are linkable to ANS open data through the school code (plesso).

Name: Observatório de Trajetos dos Estudantes do Ensino Secundário
Data Collection Frequency: Yearly (or more than once per year)
Sample Type\/Size [Other Cases, comment: All schools in the country are invited to participate in the statistical operations taking place in this context, but not all register (the registration rate varies approximately between 75% and 90%). The survey is voluntary and requires authorization, so not all students in registered schools complete the survey (the response rate for students in registered schools varies depending on the survey, approximately between 55% and 70%). If we consider the response rate for the total school population for the target group, it varies between 50% and 60%). In any case, I recommend consulting the more detailed publications for all available surveys on the DGEEC website: https://www.dgeec.medu.pt/art/6499de939eff36f307f07bdc/65524fc0455255473193d2bd/65524fef455255473193d2bf/652ec184bd5c2b00958292b5
Data Accessibility: On a computer belonging to the producer of the data, which is not connected to internet or to another computer (in this case the producer examines all the results outputs)
Access to Micro-Data [Other]: There is a need to be present at the physical facilities of the DGEEC (the data owner) to access micro-data.
Additional notes on the dataset (strengh:
Note for the question “Frequency of data collection (also in case of cross-sectional assessments)”:
OTES contains data collected at three different moments - upon entry into secondary (grade 10), at the end (grade 12) and 14 months after the end. The first moment of data collection occurred in the academic years 2007/2008, 2010/2011, 2013/2014 and 2016/2017. The second one was carried out during the academic years 2008/2009, 2009/2010, 2011/2012, 2012/2013, 2014/2015, 2015/2016, 2017/2018, 2018/2019, and 2020/2021. The third moment of data collection was from 2010 to 2022, except in the years 2012, 2015, 2018, 2021.

The methodological approach used encompasses two perspectives: cross-sectional and longitudinal:
1.	The cross-sectional analysis provides a general overview of the trajectories of secondary students at a specific moment in time, across various education and training offerings.
2.	The longitudinal analysis allows for the examination of students' trajectories over time, based on the longitudinal analysis of cohorts and semi-cohorts, relying on surveying the same group of students at different points in their educational journey.

Regarding the "Young People in Post-Secondary Education" survey, there is greater complexity, as it is conducted outside of school and sent via email. Access to the survey requires authorization and consent to fill it out, in accordance with current regulations, which sometimes makes it difficult to respond. In this sense, this survey has a lower response rate compared to the number of young people eligible to respond. It is important to clarify that the target audience for this survey consists of students who agreed to provide their email address when completing the "Students at the Exit from Secondary Education" and/or "Students at the Entrance to Secondary Education" surveys. Due to the methodology of application (sent by email 14 months later), in most years, the number of responses exceeds the typical standard for this type of application methodology.
Each of the three surveys has its own database, so in practice, the OTES inherently has not just one database, but three databases. Most of the information collected in each survey differs from one survey to another.
Assessment Type [Other]: In the 10th and 12th grade, the surveys are conducted on paper, and 14 months later, they are conducted via email (computer-based).
Other information on students (specify)::
Note in the question “Belonging to a recognised ethnic minority”: there is a variable described as “ethno-national origin”. 

Note in the question “Attitude or motivation toward education/school”: this database has questions related to the students’ opinions and satisfaction level toward school and/or school courses.
Other information on parents or househol: Other informations related to parents are described in the database's codebook.
Other information on teachers (specify):: There are no variables on this dataset about teachers.
Other information on the school or class: Other informations related to school or class are described in the database's codebook.
